<objective>
Add an isEmpty utility function to the demo project. This function should handle null, undefined, empty string, empty array, and empty object checks.
</objective>

<tasks>

<task type="auto" executor="codex" confidence="high">
  <name>Task 1: Add isEmpty utility function</name>
  <files>src/utils.js</files>
  <action>
    Add an isEmpty function to src/utils.js that returns true for null, undefined, empty string (''), empty array ([]), and empty object ({}). Export it alongside the existing capitalize function.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>node -e "const {isEmpty} = require('./src/utils.js'); console.log(isEmpty(null), isEmpty(''), isEmpty([]), isEmpty({}))"</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>src/utils.js exports isEmpty. Running `node -e "const {isEmpty} = require('./src/utils.js'); console.log(isEmpty(null), isEmpty(''), isEmpty([]), isEmpty({}))"` prints 'true true true true'.</done>
</task>

<task type="auto" executor="claude" confidence="high">
  <name>Task 2: Review isEmpty implementation</name>
  <files>src/utils.js</files>
  <action>
    Review the isEmpty implementation for edge cases. Verify it handles all specified types correctly.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>node -e "const {isEmpty} = require('./src/utils.js'); console.log(typeof isEmpty === 'function')"</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>isEmpty handles null, undefined, empty string, empty array, empty object correctly.</done>
</task>

</tasks>

<verification>
1. src/utils.js exports isEmpty function
2. isEmpty(null) returns true
3. isEmpty(undefined) returns true
4. isEmpty('') returns true
5. isEmpty([]) returns true
6. isEmpty({}) returns true
